commands.name: Get-ChildItem
commands.description: Lists files and folders in a directory.
commands.notes: Use -Recurse to search subdirectories. Use -Hidden to show hidden files.

commands.name: Set-Location
commands.description: Changes the current working directory.
commands.notes: Use 'cd -' to go to previous directory. 'cd ~' goes to home.

commands.name: New-Item
commands.description: Creates a new file, folder, or registry key.
commands.notes: -ItemType can be 'File', 'Directory', or 'SymbolicLink'

commands.name: Remove-Item
commands.description: Deletes files, folders, or registry keys.
commands.notes: Use -Recurse to delete non-empty folders. Use -Force to override.

commands.name: Copy-Item
commands.description: Copies a file or folder from one location to another.
commands.notes: Use -Recurse to copy folders. Use -Verbose to see progress.

commands.name: Move-Item
commands.description: Moves or renames a file or folder.
commands.notes: Renaming is just moving within the same directory.

commands.name: Rename-Item
commands.description: Renames a file, folder, or registry key.
commands.notes: Cannot move items across directories; use Move-Item for that.

commands.name: Get-Content
commands.description: Reads the contents of a file and displays them.
commands.notes: Use -Tail to read last N lines (like tail -f). Use -Wait to follow.

commands.name: Set-Content
commands.description: Writes new content to a file, replacing existing content.
commands.notes: Unlike Out-File, Set-Content uses string encoding by default.

commands.name: Add-Content
commands.description: Appends content to a file without replacing existing content.
commands.notes: Creates the file if it doesn't exist.

commands.name: Get-Location
commands.description: Displays the current working directory path.
commands.notes: Use 'gl -Stack' to see the location stack.

commands.name: Clear-Host
commands.description: Clears the terminal screen.
commands.notes: Does not clear scrollback buffer.

commands.name: Get-Process
commands.description: Lists running processes on the system.
commands.notes: Use -Id to filter by PID. Use -IncludeUserName to see owners.

commands.name: Stop-Process
commands.description: Terminates a running process by name or PID.
commands.notes: Use -Force to forcefully terminate. Use with caution.

commands.name: Start-Process
commands.description: Launches a new process (program, script, or file).
commands.notes: Use -Verb RunAs to run as administrator.

commands.name: Get-Service
commands.description: Gets the status of services on the system.
commands.notes: Use -ComputerName to check remote machines.

commands.name: Start-Service
commands.description: Starts one or more stopped services.
commands.notes: Requires admin privileges for most services.

commands.name: Stop-Service
commands.description: Stops one or more running services.
commands.notes: Requires admin privileges for most services.

commands.name: Test-Connection
commands.description: Sends ICMP echo requests to test network connectivity.
commands.notes: Use -Count to set number of pings. Use -Quiet for boolean output.

commands.name: Test-NetConnection
commands.description: Tests network connectivity with ping, port, and route tracing.
commands.notes: Use -Port to test a specific TCP port. Use -TraceRoute for hop tracing.

commands.name: Resolve-DnsName
commands.description: Resolves a hostname to an IP address via DNS.
commands.notes: Use -Type to query specific record types (A, MX, TXT, etc.).

commands.name: Get-NetIPAddress
commands.description: Shows IP address configuration for all network adapters.
commands.notes: Use 'ipconfig /flushdns' through PowerShell to flush DNS cache.

commands.name: Get-NetAdapter
commands.description: Lists all network adapters and their status.
commands.notes: Use -Name to filter by adapter name.

commands.name: Invoke-WebRequest
commands.description: Makes HTTP/HTTPS requests to web services.
commands.notes: Use -Method for different HTTP methods. Use -OutFile to save response.

commands.name: Invoke-RestMethod
commands.description: Makes REST API requests with JSON/XML parsing.
commands.notes: Automatically parses JSON/XML responses into PowerShell objects.

commands.name: Get-ComputerInfo
commands.description: Shows detailed system and OS information.
commands.notes: Returns comprehensive system properties.

commands.name: Get-Date
commands.description: Displays the current date and time.
commands.notes: Use -Format with .NET format strings. Use -UFormat with Unix format.

commands.name: Get-WmiObject
commands.description: Queries WMI for system information (legacy).
commands.notes: Use Get-CimInstance instead for newer systems.

commands.name: Get-CimInstance
commands.description: Queries CIM/WMI for system information (modern).
commands.notes: Modern replacement for Get-WmiObject.

commands.name: Get-HotFix
commands.description: Lists installed Windows updates and hotfixes.
commands.notes: Only shows updates installed via Windows Update or MSI.

commands.name: Get-Volume
commands.description: Shows all mounted volumes and their properties.
commands.notes: Shows drive letters, file systems, size, and free space.

commands.name: Select-String
commands.description: Searches text content using regex patterns.
commands.notes: Use -CaseSensitive for exact matching. Use -NotMatch to exclude.

commands.name: Where-Object
commands.description: Filters objects from a pipeline based on conditions.
commands.notes: Use script blocks { } for complex conditions.

commands.name: ForEach-Object
commands.description: Processes each item in a pipeline, one at a time.
commands.notes: $_ represents the current pipeline object.

commands.name: Sort-Object
commands.description: Sorts objects by one or more properties.
commands.notes: Use -Descending for reverse order. Use -Unique for distinct values.

commands.name: Group-Object
commands.description: Groups objects that share common property values.
commands.notes: Shows count and grouping details.

commands.name: Measure-Object
commands.description: Calculates numeric statistics (min, max, sum, avg, count).
commands.notes: Use -Average, -Sum, -Minimum, -Maximum for specific stats.

commands.name: Format-Table
commands.description: Formats output as a table with optional column selection.
commands.notes: Use -AutoSize for better column widths. Use -Wrap for wrapped text.

commands.name: Format-List
commands.description: Formats output as a list with one property per line.
commands.notes: Use '*', for all properties. Great for detailed views.

commands.name: Format-Wide
commands.description: Formats output as a multi-column grid.
commands.notes: Use -Column to control grid width.

commands.name: Get-Help
commands.description: Shows help documentation for PowerShell commands.
commands.notes: Use -Detailed for full help. Use -Examples to show only examples.

commands.name: Get-Command
commands.description: Lists all available commands, functions, and aliases.
commands.notes: Use -Verb and -Noun to filter. Use -Module to see module commands.

commands.name: Get-Member
commands.description: Shows the properties and methods of an object.
commands.notes: Pipe any object to gm to see what you can do with it.

commands.name: Get-Alias
commands.description: Lists or resolves PowerShell aliases.
commands.notes: Use -Definition to find aliases that point to a specific command.

commands.name: New-Alias
commands.description: Creates a new alias for a command or function.
commands.notes: Aliases only last for the current session unless added to profile.

commands.name: Set-Alias
commands.description: Creates or changes an alias (overwrites existing).
commands.notes: Use Export-Alias to save aliases to file.

commands.name: Get-Variable
commands.description: Lists all variables in the current scope.
commands.notes: Use -Scope to see variables in specific scopes.

commands.name: Set-Variable
commands.description: Creates or sets the value of a variable.
commands.notes: Use $variable = value as a shorthand.

commands.name: Export-Csv
commands.description: Exports pipeline objects to a CSV file.
commands.notes: Use -NoTypeInfo to omit the type header line.

commands.name: Import-Csv
commands.description: Imports a CSV file as PowerShell objects.
commands.notes: Headers become property names of the resulting objects.

commands.name: ConvertTo-Json
commands.description: Converts PowerShell objects to JSON format.
commands.notes: Use -Depth for nested objects. Use -Compress for minified output.

commands.name: ConvertFrom-Json
commands.description: Converts JSON strings to PowerShell objects.
commands.notes: The JSON input can be a string or file content.

commands.name: Get-ExecutionPolicy
commands.description: Shows the PowerShell script execution policy.
commands.notes: Returns Restricted, RemoteSigned, AllSigned, or Unrestricted.

commands.name: Set-ExecutionPolicy
commands.description: Sets the PowerShell script execution policy.
commands.notes: Requires admin by default. Use -Scope CurrentUser for non-admin.

commands.name: Get-Acl
commands.description: Gets the Access Control List (permissions) for a file/folder.
commands.notes: Shows owner, group, and access rules.

commands.name: Set-Acl
commands.description: Sets permissions on a file/folder using an ACL object.
commands.notes: Requires admin for system-protected items.

commands.name: Get-Date
commands.description: Returns the current date and time with formatting options.
commands.notes: Use -DisplayHint Date/Time/DateTime to control output.

commands.name: Start-Sleep
commands.description: Pauses execution for a specified duration.
commands.notes: Use -Milliseconds for sub-second delays.

commands.name: Measure-Command
commands.description: Measures how long a script or command takes to run.
commands.notes: Returns a TimeSpan object with TotalSeconds, etc.

commands.name: Get-ChildItem Env:
commands.description: Lists all environment variables.
commands.notes: Access individual vars: $env:VARNAME

commands.name: Set-Item Env:
commands.description: Sets an environment variable value.
commands.notes: Only affects the current process. Use [Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable for permanent.

commands.name: Get-PSDrive
commands.description: Lists all available drives (file system + PowerShell drives).
commands.notes: Includes registry (HKLM, HKCU), certificate, and function drives.

commands.name: Get-Module
commands.description: Lists loaded and available PowerShell modules.
commands.notes: Use -ListAvailable to see installable modules.

commands.name: Import-Module
commands.description: Loads a PowerShell module into the current session.
commands.notes: Modules can be loaded from any path.

commands.name: Install-Module
commands.description: Installs a module from the PowerShell Gallery.
commands.notes: Requires NuGet provider. Use -Scope CurrentUser to avoid admin.

commands.name: Update-Module
commands.description: Updates installed PowerShell modules to latest version.
commands.notes: Without -Name, updates all modules.

commands.name: Find-Module
commands.description: Searches the PowerShell Gallery for modules.
commands.notes: Search supports wildcards.

commands.name: New-Module
commands.description: Creates an in-memory module with custom functions.
commands.notes: Use -ReturnResult to export module members.

commands.name: Write-Output
commands.description: Sends output to the pipeline or console.
commands.notes: Implicit output is the same as Write-Output.

commands.name: Write-Host
commands.description: Writes directly to the console (not to pipeline).
commands.notes: Use -ForegroundColor and -BackgroundColor for colors.

commands.name: Write-Progress
commands.description: Displays a progress bar in the console.
commands.notes: Use in loops to show long-running operation progress.

commands.name: Write-Verbose
commands.description: Outputs verbose log messages (requires -Verbose switch).
commands.notes: Only visible when running with -Verbose common parameter.

commands.name: Write-Debug
commands.description: Outputs debug messages to the console.
commands.notes: Only visible when $DebugPreference is Continue or Inquire.

commands.name: Write-Error
commands.description: Writes an error to the error stream.
commands.notes: Does not terminate the script. Use throw for terminating errors.
